title: Nurture and "GROW": The Fourth Step in Inversing, ('Ground, Reinforce, Observe, Water'). Abrahams, S.P. Flourishing Future Foundation.
author: Abrahams, Sean Paul
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Inversing—the transition from a NARMA state (Negativity, Apathy, Restriction, Meaninglessness, Adversity) to a PERMA state (Positivity, Engagement, Relationships, Meaning, Achievement)—requires more than recognizing, understanding, and replacing cognitive patterns. Once the T in TURN (Target) has been addressed through the mnemonic protocol “NAME”, the U (Understand the Path) has been activated through “PATH”, and R (Replace and Swap) has solidified new thought patterns through “SWAP”, the final and most crucial step is N – Nurture and Grow. 
 This phase is about reinforcing flourishing states so they become second nature. Dr. Carol Dweck’s Growth Mindset research (Dweck, 2006) has demonstrated that people who nurture small successes through effort and reinforcement build long-term resilience. If a flourishing state is not consciously reinforced, the brain will default back to its most rehearsed cognitive state, often leading to relapse into NARMA inertia. 
 The GROW mnemonic in the last step of TURN, Nurture stands for Ground, Reinforce, Observe, Water. This ensures that individuals don’t just experience flourishing states temporarily but actively cultivate them into a sustainable identity shift.
steps_raw:
